chief_complaint: A 37-year-old man presented with a one-week history of headache, fever, and chills.
history: The patient had a one-week history of headache, fever, and chills. He had a medical history of gout, diagnosed three years earlier. He experienced an acute gout flare during hospitalization, which required antigout therapy.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: CSF analysis: lymphocytic pleocytosis, elevated protein, mildly decreased glucose, and ADA activity of 7.4 IU/L and 9.1 IU/L. Serum ADA levels were also elevated (14.7 IU/L). CSF PCR confirmed VZV meningitis.
